label: Figure 1
title: Comparison of impulsivity scores between healthy controls (HC), OCD patients (OCD), and OCD with behavioural addiction (OCD_BA)
sourceCaption: Rai et al., 2022 — caption states (p < 0.001) for group comparison.
reconstructionNote: Reconstructed from paper text (means ± SD). Error bars show ±1 SD as reported; verify visual style against original figure.
analysisNote: Paper: one-way ANOVA for BIS-11 total across three groups; F and post-hoc details not in extracted text.

series.nReported: OCD without BA vs with BA split elsewhere; totals n=150 OCD
n=105 OCD with BA per Table 4

interpretation: BIS-11 total increases from HC to OCD to OCD with co-occurring BA, with overall ANOVA p < 0.001. Supports hypothesis that impulsivity is highest in the OCD+BA subgroup and higher in OCD overall than controls.
